I am leaning casually against the door of a white 1986 Testarossa, wearing a sharp, tailored blazer in a soft peach color with shoulder pads, paired with white linen trousers and no socks in loafers. My black and white hair is slicked back, and I’m wearing dark sunglasses even though it’s night. I’m looking off into the distance with a intense, brooding stare, one hand resting on the car door. Palm trees are silhouetted against the glowing sky in the background. The vibe is cool, calculated, and undeniably 80s—pure Don Johnson energy but with a twist.
